close_reason: Rewrote get-without-field to use Value::take() avoiding double allocation
description:
lib.rs:220-252: in the no-field path, the response body is parsed via serde_json::from_slice into a serde_json::Value, navigated, then serialized BACK to a Vec<u8> via serde_json::to_vec. This double-traverses the JSON and double-allocates the data map.

Impact: not in a hot path (one round-trip per get), but for large secrets (configs, multi-MB blobs) it is 2× the necessary memory residency in non-Zeroizing heap memory and 2× CPU.

The doc comment at lib.rs:51-55 acknowledges that response bytes are already in non-Zeroizing memory before parsing, so parsing with serde_json adds no zeroization risk. The efficiency point stands separately.

Fix: secretx-core has json_navigate / json_extract_string_field byte-level scanners (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:592, 163). Use them to navigate to data.data and slice the underlying bytes directly into the output SecretValue, avoiding the intermediate Value tree.

P3 efficiency because the savings only matter for atypically large secrets.
title: Double-allocation in get-without-field — parse to Value then re-serialize
